evidence_old: CORRECTS AN INEI LABELLING ERROR. The 2006 .dta labels p1142 as {0: pase, 2: celular}, but the released data contains NO code 2 in any year 2004-2011 — it holds 0 and 1. Four independent lines say code 1 is ownership: the official 2006 Diccionario states '1 Celular' with 'Rango : 0 - 1'; the .dta's OWN label in 2004/2005 and 2008+ is {0: pase, 1: celular}; zero rows sit at code 2; and weighted ownership ramps smoothly 16.4/20.7/29.8/45.0/59.7/67.0/73.1/75.2% through 2006, which a mislabel cannot bend.
evidence_new: PHANTOM CODE IN THE 2006 LABEL. The 2006 .dta labels p1142 with code(s) [2], which appear in NO year of the released data — INEI wrote the variable's position in its battery as the value code. The years whose labels match the data ([2004, 2005, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2020, 2024, 2025]) label code 1 as 'telefono celular'.

evidence_old: THE DICTIONARY IS WRONG, NOT THE DATA. INEI's 2007 Diccionario declares 'P107A14: 0 No / 2 Si / Rango : 0, 2', but the released data holds 0 and 1 — code 2 never appears in any year 2007-2011. BEHAVIOURAL PROOF: this question asks whether the dwelling was modified and the NEXT question asks how many m2. Code-1 rows answer the m2 follow-up 99.7-99.9% of the time; code-0 rows answer it 0.0%, in every year. Only 'Si' opens that follow-up, so code 1 is the affirmative and code 0 is the negative.
evidence_new: MULTIPLE-RESPONSE FLAG, proven by structure. p107a14 holds only {0,1} in every year and its label names exactly one code — the affirmative ({1: 'no gasto ampliación'}). That is how a multiple-response battery is stored: the label says what the flag means WHEN SET, and 0 is 'not marked'. No year labels code 0 otherwise, and no other value is available to it.

evidence_new: INEI'S OWN TESTIMONY ABOUT ITS OWN CODE. 2007 ships no label for p107a14 code 1, but INEI labels that exact code on that exact variable in [2008] as 'no gasto ampliación' — in its own .dta value labels and/or its published Diccionario de Datos. Same variable, same code, same concept: INEI stopped DOCUMENTING the code, it did not stop USING it.
